breaking.text: Veeva Systems jumps 9% after hours on Q4 earnings and revenue beat
Broadcom beats Q1 estimates, guides Q2 revenue to $22B; AI revenue soars 106% to $8.4B — stock up 5% after hours
Senate war powers vote fails, allowing Trump to continue military strikes on Iran without new congressional authorization
Broadcom beats Q1 estimates: revenue $19.3B (+29% YoY), AI revenue $8.4B (+106%), guides Q2 to $22B — stock jumps 5% after hours
CEO Hock Tan projects $100B+ AI chip revenue pipeline citing Anthropic, Meta, and OpenAI as key customers

summary: Wall Street staged a broad recovery on Wednesday as easing oil prices and strong economic data offset lingering Middle East war jitters. The S&P 500 rose 0.78% to 6,869.50, the Nasdaq surged 1.29%, and the Dow added 238 points. The ISM Services PMI surprised to the upside at 56.1% — the strongest reading since 2022 — while ADP private payrolls topped estimates at 63,000. After the bell, Broadcom delivered a blowout quarter with AI revenue up 106% to $8.4B and guided Q2 revenue to $22B, well above consensus, sending shares up 5% in extended trading. Consumer discretionary led sectors during the session with Ross Stores surging 7% on strong Q4 results, Moderna jumped 11% after settling its COVID vaccine patent dispute for up to $2.25B, and Coinbase rallied 14% as Bitcoin reclaimed $73,000 on Trump crypto policy tailwinds.

highlights.text: Moderna surges 11% after settling $2.25B COVID vaccine patent dispute with Arbutus/Genevant, removing key legal overhang and securing royalty-free LNP license
Okta tops Q4 estimates as AI agent security demand accelerates — EPS $0.90 vs $0.85 expected, revenue $761M vs $749M — stock up 3% after hours
Veeva Systems beats Q4 estimates handily — EPS $2.06 vs $1.93 expected, revenue $836M vs $811M — shares up 9% after hours
Broadcom Q2 guidance of $22B revenue crushes $20.56B consensus; CEO Hock Tan sees 'line of sight' to $100B+ in AI chip revenue by 2027; announces $10B buyback
ISM Services PMI surges to 56.1% in February — strongest expansion since 2022 — with New Orders jumping 5.5 points to 58.6% and Prices Paid cooling to 63.0%
KeyBanc upgrades Dow Inc. and LyondellBasell to Overweight, citing 11-15% of global ethylene capacity disrupted by Iran conflict benefiting U.S. petrochemical producers
Iran officially denies NYT report of CIA back-channel peace outreach, calling it 'psychological warfare' — oil prices tick higher on the denial
ADP reports 63,000 private-sector jobs added in February, strongest in seven months, beating 43K consensus — but January revised sharply down to just 11,000
Bitcoin breaks above $73,000 to a one-month high as crypto rallies broadly — Solana +7.2%, Chainlink +8% — though traders warn of potential bull trap
Broadcom Q1 AI semiconductor revenue surged 106% YoY to $8.4B; CEO Hock Tan forecasts AI chip revenue exceeding $100B by 2027 and announces $10B buyback
Dubai stocks crash 4.7% — worst since 2022 — as UAE markets reopen after two-day closure following Iranian drone and missile strikes
Coinbase jumps 12% after Trump publicly backs crypto firms over banks on stablecoin yield, urging passage of digital asset market structure bill
BofA initiates Tesla at Buy with $460 price target, calling it the clear leader in autonomous driving — TSLA gains 3.6% on the session
Ross Stores surges 7.4% after Q4 EPS of $2.00 beats estimates; comp sales up 9% — plans 110 new stores in 2026 and 10% dividend hike
Broadcom Q1 revenue hits record $19.3B (+29% YoY), AI revenue surges 106% to $8.4B; guides Q2 to $22B with AI revenue expected at $10.7B — stock up 5% after hours
Bank of America initiates Tesla at Buy with $460 price target, citing robotaxi and autonomous driving upside
CoreWeave jumps 8% after securing multi-year deal to power Perplexity's AI inference on Nvidia Grace Blackwell clusters
GitLab plunges 13.5% to 52-week low of $23.41 after weak FY2027 guidance and fears that AI coding agents threaten its core business
Fed Beige Book shows weaker consumer weighing on sales across multiple districts, with sticky price pressures from energy and labor costs
Broadcom guides Q2 revenue to $22B, crushing $20.56B consensus; announces $10B share buyback program; stock up 5% after hours
